questions.question: Burhachapori Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Assam Government is planning to create a new Reserved Forest adjacent to Burhachapori Wildlife Sanctuary in Sonitpur district, Assam, to strengthen conservation efforts. Burhachapori Wildlife Sanctuary is located on the south bank of the Brahmaputra River, Assam and is part of the Laokhowa–Burhachapori ecosystem. It lies strategically between Kaziranga National Park (east), Orang National Park (west), and Laokhowa Wildlife Sanctuary (south). The region experiences a sub-tropical monsoon climate, supporting rich biodiversity.

questions.question: Litani River, that was recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Recently, Israeli airstrikes destroyed Qasmiyeh Bridge over Litani River in Lebanon’s southern coastal highway. Litani River is the longest and major river of Lebanon. It originates west of Baalbek and flows southwest through Bekaa Valley between Lebanon and Anti-Lebanon mountains. It finally drains into the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.question: World Meteorological Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Meteorological Day is observed every year on March 23 to highlight the importance of weather, climate and water science. It commemorates the establishment of World Meteorological Organization (WMO – World Meteorological Organization) in 1950. The 2026 theme “Observing Today, Protecting Tomorrow” emphasizes accurate data and continuous monitoring. It promotes use of advanced technologies like satellites and radar for climate prediction and disaster management.

questions.question: Tele-MANAS is an initiative of which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: Tele-MANAS (Tele Mental Health Assistance and Networking Across States) has established 53 cells across 36 States and Union Territories and provides services in 20 languages. The helpline has handled more than 34.34 lakh calls, reflecting its wide reach and usage. The Tele-MANAS mobile application was launched on 10 October 2024 by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare to provide comprehensive mental health support from well-being to disorders. The programme provides multiple referrals including counselling, clinical evaluation, Mental Health Professionals (MHPs – Mental Health Professionals), and emergency psychiatric care.

questions.question: The book “Janjati Gaurav” has been published by which institute?

options.label: B
options.text: Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts

answer.label: B
answer.text: Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts

questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA – Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts) published the book “Janjati Gaurav” to document tribal history and culture. The book highlights contributions of tribal communities in freedom struggle and nation-building. It includes archival records, research and stories of tribal leaders and movements. It mentions Mangarh Dham linked to uprising led by Govind Guru and sacrifices of tribal fighters. Ministry of Tribal Affairs approved 11 Tribal Freedom Fighters Museums across 10 states.
